mnemonics.title: Pure Virtual Function Declaration
mnemonics.content: VIRTUAL = 0: Remember pure virtual uses = 0 syntax (like ZERO implementation = pure)

mnemonics.title: Abstract Class Properties
mnemonics.content: ABC: Abstract class = Cannot instantiate, Base class, Can have implemented functions

mnemonics.title: Virtual Destructor Rule
mnemonics.content: DESTRUCTOR always VIRTUAL in abstract: When in doubt, make it virtual to prevent leaks

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Table - lookup table for dynamic function dispatch
acronyms.usage: Used by compiler to resolve virtual function calls at runtime
Mechanism to determine object type at runtime, used with dynamic_cast and typeid
Class containing pure virtual functions, serves as interface
Design principle: prefer many specific interfaces over one general interface

visualTips.visualization: Draw function signature with = 0 as an 'anchor' or 'anchor point' that must be 'filled' by derived classes
Draw an inverted triangle: abstract base at top, derived concrete classes at bottom - showing IS-A relationship
Think of vtable as a 'dispatch center' - when call comes, it looks up which actual function to call based on object type
Imagine slicing the top off an ice cream cone - you lose the bottom (derived) portion when assigned to base

analogies.analogy: Like a job contract template - defines what must be done but not HOW to do it. Each derived class 'fills in' the details.
Like a blueprint for a house - shows the plan and structure but you can't live in the blueprint itself.
Like having a master key that can unlock all locks - virtual destructor ensures all 'locks' (destructors) are properly closed.
Like a remote control specification - defines what buttons exist and what they should do, but doesn't specify the internal electronics.
